description:
MERGED with .3 (DeriveTypeDef and the resolver are one unit; the TypeDefined fact closes the loop between them). Deliver:

(1) Core resolver resolve_type_expr(namespace, TypeExpr) -> a hard compiler2 Ty. Scalars/builtins minted directly; structure (list/tuple/union/arrow/struct-record) via compiler2 Types; a Name classified against the captured namespace -- a Type binding -> TypeName -> read TypeDefined; an unbound lowercase name -> a free type var; a builtin -> a scalar. Parametric @types instantiated by substitution.

HARD CONSTRAINT: pure compiler2 Types through the single interner (world.types_mut()). NEVER legacy crate::types, NEVER a throwaway crate::types::new(), NEVER a legacy_type_test_ty-style re-projection or type_is_X if-ladder. That pattern is radioactive (see fz-rh2.13).

(2) FactKey::TypeDefined(TypeName) carrying Presence + a TypeName -> Ty(/parametric template) store, like FunctionContractMap.

(3) Job::DeriveTypeDef: strictly PULLED, never queued at note time. Waits on the @type body recorded refs (.12) TypeDefined, then resolves the body (Plain / Refines / Opaque -- brands minted inner-in-symbol via mint_brand(inner,name)) and publishes. A @type no reached consumer references stays cold like an uncalled function.

(4) resolve_spec(namespace, SpecDecl) -> crate::specs::ResolvedSpec (Ty + ResolvedTypeShape) over the core resolver, for .4 to wire into contract/dispatch/extern and .10 to parity-test.

Verify: a referenced @type publishes TypeDefined once, an unreferenced one stays cold; resolve_type_expr over the worked examples (incl. a refines brand and a parametric instantiation); resolve_spec over foo(SomeModule.t(float), integer) :: x when x: SomeModule.t; no crate::types::new() anywhere on the compiler2 resolver path.

comments.text: Split: REFERENCE moved to fz-rh2.12.12 (now blocks .2). DeriveTypeDef reads its wait-set from fz-rh2.12.12 recorded refs for the @type body, waits on those TypeDefined, then resolves.

title: DeriveTypeDef formula + TypeDefined fact + TypeName->Ty store
